fields.description:
## Reproduction Steps

1. On origin/main with `[identity] require_authenticated = true` (Gerrit change 759 / commit 8bb261c19), push any commit.
2. The `verify-identity` merge-gate (`.github/workflows/verify-identity.yaml`, post-merge on the GitHub mirror) runs `rebar verify-identity` over the whole store.
3. Observe: `verify-identity: FAIL — 182 enforced in-scope event(s) are not verified` — every push to main goes red.

**Expected:** with write-signing rolled out, the whole store verifies clean under enforcement.
**Actual:** 182 enforced events fail: 2 unsigned `COMPLETION_VERDICT` sidecars + 180 `key_not_valid_at_era`.

## Root cause (both experimentally confirmed)

- **Source 1 — reducer-ignored sidecars are wrongly in the gate's scope.** `COMPLETION_VERDICT` (and siblings `REVIEW_RESULT`/`TICKET_DIGEST`/`ENQUEUE_ENRICH`) are NOT in `reducer.KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES` (non-state-bearing observability), yet `verify_authorship._event_from_file` classifies them. Emitted best-effort-unsigned when no signing key resolves, they become enforced failures. This is a mechanism-2 artifact (completion verification) whose store event fell into mechanism-1 (identity write-signing) scope.
- **Source 2 — compaction persists `commit_sha: null`.** `compact._build_authorship_ledger` records `null` when `resolve_event_commit` returns None at fold time and retires the raw file; the gate can't re-resolve the null, fail-closes it to ENFORCED, and mis-classifies validly-signed, era-valid events as `key_not_valid_at_era` (`verify_authorship.py` :206-220,:249-270).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `verify_authorship._event_from_file` skips events whose `event_type` is not in `reducer.KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES` (reducer-ignored sidecars), proven by a test where a `COMPLETION_VERDICT` on a work ticket is NOT an enforced failure while a KNOWN unsigned `STATUS`/`CREATE` still IS. (`src/rebar/_commands/verify_authorship.py`)
- [ ] The gate re-resolves a LEDGER entry whose recorded `commit_sha` is null via `authorship.resolve_position_commit(position, tracker)` before the era/enforcement decision, proven by a test that a null-commit, era-valid signed ledger entry classifies `verified` not `key_not_valid_at_era`. (`src/rebar/_commands/verify_authorship.py`)
- [ ] `compact._build_authorship_ledger` never persists `commit_sha: null` when the position is resolvable — it falls back to `resolve_position_commit`; proven by a monkeypatched unit test. (`src/rebar/_commands/compact.py`)
- [ ] Regression tests added under `tests/unit/` and the full suite passes; `make lint` clean.
- [ ] [operator-attested] `verify-identity` is added as a pre-merge job in the Gerrit `Verified` gate (`.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml`) so the whole-store enforcement check runs BEFORE merge, not only post-merge (mitigates the prior epics' gap).

## Follow-on (separate change; NOT an acceptance criterion of this bug)

Re-enabling `identity.require_authenticated = true` on main (with a freshly pinned
`enforce_since`) is the deployment step this fix ENABLES, tracked as its own change. It is
deliberately not gated on this bug's closure: its safety is proven by that change's OWN
pre-merge whole-store `verify-identity` gate (added here), which reports 0 enforced-not-verified
before it can merge — the self-correcting whole-store measurement the previous rollout lacked.

## Context

Remediation of the incomplete identity-signing rollout (epics e68d built, dc09 rolled out). Prior epics verified via a producer-side window scan + force-closes and never ran the whole-store-under-enforcement check; this bug fixes the two escaped defect classes and hardens the process. Rollback of the premature flip landed as Gerrit change 767.
